title: ferray-polynomial: fitting uses internal normal equations instead of ferray-linalg lstsq
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

fitting.rs implements least-squares fitting via the normal equations (V^T W V c = V^T W y) with its own matrix multiplication and solve. NumPy uses np.linalg.lstsq (SVD-based) which is more numerically stable, especially for ill-conditioned Vandermonde matrices (high polynomial degrees). ferray-linalg has lstsq backed by faer. Delegate to ferray-linalg::lstsq for the solve step, keeping the Vandermonde construction in ferray-polynomial.
